deductions.reason: Across all three transcripts, the tutor never explicitly explains why it is guiding rather than giving answers—e.g., never says something like 'I'm asking you to work this out yourself because active reasoning builds retention.' The tutoring constraints are consistently applied but the rationale for them is never articulated to the student.

In transcripts 2 and 3, students make clear improvements (e.g., T2 Turn 9 student articulates a strong rule; T3 Turn 8 student correctly identifies means vs. side effect). The tutor moves forward with the next task but does not prompt reflection on what changed in the student's thinking or why the improved approach worked.

grade.overview: The tutor performs strongly across all three transcripts. Socratic method is consistently applied—no direct answers are given, key ideas are student-led, and the tutor scaffolds well by building on student progress and using targeted diagnostic questions. Dialogue quality is excellent: the tutor avoids redundancy, stays anchored to the assignment, and redirects appropriately. Communication is concise and supportive. Two deductions apply: the tutor never explains the rationale for its guided approach (1.3.A.a), and after student breakthroughs the tutor moves forward without prompting reflection on what changed (1.3.C.a). The 1.3.B criterion is borderline—the tutor does give process-level feedback implicitly—but the feedback is sufficiently method-oriented to avoid a deduction.
grade.judge_reasoning: 1.1: No submission-ready content is provided, no rewriting of student text occurs, and key ideas are consistently generated by students before the tutor confirms or redirects. Full marks. 1.2: The tutor builds on correct student steps in every transcript (e.g., T1 Turn 5 affirming the Switch structure, T2 Turn 7 confirming the Fuel classification) and uses diagnostic questions before addressing confusion. Full marks. 1.3: The tutoring rationale is never explained to students—the tutor simply enforces the Socratic approach without ever saying why this benefits learning (1.3.A.a, -2). After clear student improvements (T2 Turn 9, T3 Turn 8), the tutor advances the task without prompting reflection on what changed in the student's thinking (1.3.C.a, -2). Process feedback is present and specific enough to avoid a 1.3.B deduction. Dialogue and communication quality are strong throughout all three transcripts with no evidence supporting deductions.
